description: Enforced scope boundaries for NP advanced practice content generation. All generators must consult this file before producing content for fenced topics. Violations must be flagged in the post-generation duplication report.

slugA_owns: all agent classes at a map level (SU, DPP-4, TZD, GLP-1, SGLT-2, insulin — listed as options only)
A1c target selection by patient profile
hypoglycemia risk stratification across agents
cost and adherence considerations
insulin initiation decision tree (when to start, basal vs prandial)
sulfonylurea and DPP-4 inhibitor comparative selection
TZD use cases and contraindications
slugA_may_not_include: mechanism of GLP-1 receptor agonism
SGLT-2 mechanism and glycosuria
specific cardiorenal trial data (EMPA-REG OUTCOME, LEADER, DECLARE-TIMI 58, CREDENCE, DAPA-CKD, DAPA-HF)
GLP-1 adverse effects: nausea, vomiting, pancreatitis, thyroid C-cell tumor risk
SGLT-2 adverse effects: UTI, DKA risk, volume depletion, Fournier gangrene
agent-level differences within GLP-1 class (semaglutide vs liraglutide vs dulaglutide)
agent-level differences within SGLT-2 class (empagliflozin vs dapagliflozin vs canagliflozin)
slugB_owns: cardiorenal indication-driven selection: T2DM + established ASCVD, T2DM + HFrEF, T2DM + CKD
specific cardiovascular and renal outcome trial data per agent class
GLP-1 class mechanism, adverse effect profile, and contraindications in detail
SGLT-2 class mechanism, adverse effect profile, and contraindications in detail
agent-level selection within GLP-1 class
agent-level selection within SGLT-2 class
overlap cases (GLP-1 preferred with ASCVD; SGLT-2 preferred with HF/CKD)
slugB_may_not_include: general T2DM step therapy decision tree
sulfonylurea or DPP-4 inhibitor comparative selection

slugA_owns: TSH interpretation: low vs normal vs high, primary vs central thyroid disease
free T4 and free T3 role and when to order
clinical presentation of hypothyroidism (cold intolerance, constipation, bradycardia, myxedema)
clinical presentation of hyperthyroidism (heat intolerance, tremor, palpitations, exophthalmos)
subclinical vs overt thresholds for treatment initiation
Graves disease vs Hashimoto vs thyroid nodule workup overview
when to initiate levothyroxine (clinical decision — TSH thresholds)
treatment goal concept (target TSH range for age group)
thyroid cancer red flags and referral criteria
referral indications to endocrinology
slugA_may_not_include: levothyroxine starting dose calculation or formula
weight-based dosing (mcg/kg/day)
administration timing (30–60 min before food)
specific absorption interactions: calcium, iron, PPIs, sucralfate, cholestyramine
drug interactions that alter levothyroxine requirements: warfarin, digoxin, rifampin, phenytoin
TSH monitoring interval after dose change
dose titration step size
slugB_owns: starting dose calculation: 1.6 mcg/kg/day full replacement; 25–50 mcg elderly/cardiac
administration timing: 30–60 min before food, bedtime alternative
absorption interactions: calcium, iron, PPIs, sucralfate, cholestyramine (take 4 hrs apart)
drug interactions that increase levothyroxine clearance or displacement: rifampin, phenytoin, carbamazepine, warfarin effect
TSH monitoring: recheck at 6–8 weeks after each dose change
dose titration: 12.5–25 mcg increments
brand vs generic switching caution
slugB_may_not_include: clinical diagnosis of hypothyroidism or hyperthyroidism

slugA_owns: CKD staging: G1–G5 by eGFR and albuminuria (KDIGO framework)
renoprotective agents: RAAS inhibitors (ACE/ARB) for albuminuria reduction
SGLT-2 inhibitors for CKD progression (CREDENCE/DAPA-CKD data summary)
blood pressure targets in CKD (<130/80 with proteinuria)
dietary management: sodium, potassium, phosphate, protein restriction thresholds
monitoring labs and frequency: eGFR, serum potassium, creatinine, urine albumin:creatinine, CBC, PTH, phosphorus
when to refer nephrology: eGFR <30 G4–G5, rapidly declining eGFR, refractory hyperkalemia
anemia of CKD: EPO deficiency concept and when to consider treatment
metabolic acidosis management in CKD: sodium bicarbonate threshold
mineral bone disease: calcium, phosphate, vitamin D overview
slugA_may_not_include: Cockcroft-Gault formula application for drug dosing
specific drugs to avoid below eGFR thresholds (NSAIDs, metformin, nitrofurantoin)
dose adjustment tables for antibiotics in CKD
DOAC dosing adjustments in CKD
contrast nephropathy prevention protocol
opioid caution in CKD with specific agents named
slugB_owns: Cockcroft-Gault creatinine clearance formula and when to use vs MDRD/CKD-EPI
high-yield drugs to avoid entirely: NSAIDs (worsen CKD), metformin (hold if eGFR <30, caution 30–45), nitrofurantoin (ineffective and toxic if eGFR <30)
antibiotic dose adjustment in CKD: fluoroquinolones, aminoglycosides, beta-lactams, vancomycin
DOAC dose adjustment: apixaban renal criteria (2 of 3: age ≥80, weight ≤60kg, Cr ≥1.5), rivaroxaban/dabigatran thresholds
opioid caution: morphine and codeine active metabolite accumulation, prefer hydromorphone
contrast nephropathy: hold metformin, NSAIDs; pre-hydration protocol; NAC role
potassium-sparing diuretics and RAAS: hyperkalemia risk monitoring intervals in CKD
slugB_may_not_include: renoprotective therapy strategies (RAAS, SGLT-2 for CKD)
CKD monitoring labs and surveillance frequency

slugA_owns: DSM-5 MDD criteria: 9 symptoms (SIGECAPS + depressed mood), 5+ for 2 weeks, functional impairment, exclusion of medical/substance cause
PHQ-9 scoring and severity staging: mild 5–9, moderate 10–14, moderately severe 15–19, severe 20–27
PHQ-9 item 9 (suicidal ideation) response protocol
Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS): ideation vs intent vs plan vs means
suicide risk stratification: low / moderate / high and corresponding clinical actions
safety planning components: warning signs, coping strategies, support contacts, means restriction
clinical decision framework: mild → watchful wait + therapy; moderate → therapy + medication option; severe → medication + therapy + close follow-up; high risk → same-day psychiatry or ED
indications to refer to psychiatry vs manage in primary care
psychotherapy options at decision level (CBT, behavioral activation — name only, not detailed)
slugA_may_not_include: comparative SSRI profiles (fluoxetine vs sertraline vs escitalopram vs paroxetine)
SNRI profiles (venlafaxine vs duloxetine)
bupropion or mirtazapine profiles
adverse effect comparison across antidepressants
CYP450 interaction profiles of specific antidepressants
antidepressant switching protocols
dose ranges for specific antidepressants
black box warning details for specific agents
slugB_owns: SSRI agent comparison: fluoxetine (long half-life, activating, CYP2D6 inhibitor), sertraline (neutral, broad indications, safest in cardiac), escitalopram (most tolerated, fewest interactions), paroxetine (most anticholinergic, shortest half-life, avoid in pregnancy, strong CYP2D6 inhibitor)
SNRI comparison: venlafaxine (dose-dependent BP elevation, discontinuation syndrome), duloxetine (pain indication, hepatotoxicity risk)
bupropion: smoking cessation, weight neutral, contraindicated with seizure disorder or eating disorders, activating
mirtazapine: sedating, appetite stimulation, useful in elderly or underweight patients, NaSSA mechanism
black box warning: suicidality risk in patients <25 years
serotonin syndrome recognition and risk combinations
switching strategies: cross-taper vs direct switch vs washout (fluoxetine 5-week washout before MAOI)
dose titration timelines and typical therapeutic dose ranges
CYP450 interaction profiles for each agent
slugB_may_not_include: decision to prescribe vs refer to psychiatry

phase1_generation_rules.description: Rules enforced during Phase 1 (Diagnostic Foundations) content generation. All 14 topics are differential diagnosis / diagnostic reasoning topics. These rules are absolute.
permitted_question_types: Which finding most supports diagnosis X over Y
Which condition is most consistent with this presentation
Which patient requires immediate referral / transfer (disposition only, not treatment)
Which workup should be ordered first
forbidden_question_types: How would you treat this condition
Appropriate follow-up interval for chronic management
phase1_generation_rules.stem_anchor_uniqueness: No two questions across Phase 1 may share the same combination of: chief complaint + patient age group + key differentiating finding. Each question must register a unique stem anchor.
phase1_generation_rules.endocrine_crisis_scope: Topic endocrine-crisis-differentials-recognition covers recognition and triage disposition ONLY. No management mechanics (no fluids, no hydrocortisone dosing, no antithyroid drug selection) may appear in questions or lesson sections.
phase1_generation_rules.recognition_vs_management_boundary: Phase 1 lessons may state what condition a presentation represents and what initial diagnostic test to order. They may NOT detail treatment protocols, prescribing decisions, or chronic management — those belong to Phase 3–4.
phase1_generation_rules.cross_topic_duplication_guard: After generating each topic's questions, check all prior question stem anchors. Flag any stem that reuses the same chief complaint + demographic + key finding combination across any two Phase 1 topics.

renamed_topic.reason: Original slug implied adrenal insufficiency management. Renamed to reflect recognition-only differential scope across AI, thyroid storm, DKA, HHS, myxedema coma, and hypoglycemic crisis.
